sentences.text: Hey there, Ben, can you hear me alright?
Yes, I can hear you well.
How's it going?
Not bad, not bad.
How's your week going?
Yeah, it's pretty good so far.
Glad to hear it, glad to hear it, especially the week after a long weekend can be a bit tough, too.
So, glad to hear everything's all right on your end.
Yeah, no, I appreciate it.
And thank you for… taking some time here to walk me through this.

you know, I… I know there's a lot of back and forth, but essentially the genesis of, you know, this whole conversation really is… Previously, when we were a client of Radix, you know, we were getting a lot of good… well, it was pretty simple, but we were getting… what our leadership felt was a lot of good push reporting, you know, to kind of delivered in their inbox weekly of… High-level metrics that… they were able to see the portfolio at a quick glance, kind of understood where we, you know, lie, and compared to our competitors.
So, I was hoping to try and get something similar through ApartmentIQ.
Right.
That makes sense.
Talk to me about… did you… have you hit those… either of those links that I sent over?
Did you take a look at the reporting in there?
Let me pull this back up.
I clicked through…
Yeah, it may have gotten lost, because I know we're on a couple threads.
Let me see… give me one second…
Yeah, I think, I'm trying to think what day… where do I have it sent?
I think it was the property, yeah, I'm pulling it up now, I think.
Yep, on September 4th, subject line, regarding ApartmentIQ portfolio level reporting.
Download.
Downloading all the property data…
Yeah, and effectively, the way I ordered it there in the email is kind of from the most high level to the most nuanced and specific unit level data.
So, the property report, very high level, effectively property summaries across every property in your comp sets.
The floor plan report goes down one level.
We're talking, literally.
data on the floor plans across the comp sets.
And then finally, the unit-level data sync, which is every single unit in every comp set across your portfolio.
Got it.
So… there's a lot of data in all of these, and sometimes I wonder, you know, are we killing you with too much information?
Is it information overload?
So that's why we have the three levels.
I'm thinking that the reporting there is more robust than what's in Radix, but I'm happy to hear your feedback there.
The thing that we can't do with these is get them delivered automatically.
That's something you would need to build through your data warehousing or through a, through an automation in Excel, for instance.
Okay.
Alright, can you give me one second, Phil?
Yeah, sure.
Sorry about that, Phil.
I, had my daycare calling me, which is never good, so I had to figure out who and… which one of them, and who was sick, blah blah blah.
How many kids do you have, then?
Two.
Two, so…
Yeah.
It's always… someone's always got something going on, so I truly apologize for that.
Not at all.
No problem.
So, let me share my screen real quick, I guess.
This might be more simple.
So, definitely the… the property data report, which, you know, we talked about as sort of the highest level report, I think that's more in line with what we're looking.
Definitely… Right.
Would, in my opinion, be data overload for them.
I think it's pro… well, yeah, definitely too much.
I mean, there's good data here, but as an executive looking at this, I know they would tell me, you know, what the hell is this?
You know, way too much to.
Sure.
Is there a way we can come up with, like, a custom report?
Yes.
We can do a custom report.
It's a bit more legwork, and I just want to be super clear.
What do you expect?
there.
It is a bit more legwork.
Let's go… maybe I can share my screen, and I'll go into the application and kind of walk us through what this would look like.
Okay.
Share screen… alright.
So…

What we do have, and just to kind of… before I even get into the solution I'm going to propose, because it is kind of a workaround to what you want to do, we are developing and rebuilding this portfolio summary page, which is going to allow us to provide For level reporting.

So, your executives will get a report that's tailored to the portfolio that matters to them at a high level, rather than engaging with this particular interface, which is outdated, doesn't have kind of the most up-to-date metrics that we've put in there.
So this kind of change is coming before the end of this year.
Got it.
So…
Now let's talk about the workaround, and I think that'll work best based on what I'm hearing from you guys, and kind of what I've heard from other former Radix users.
What we've done Is you come into the market survey here.
And through several iterations, we have made this market survey page entirely customizable.
So I can choose exactly what metrics are relevant for me based on the categories here.
whatever I'm interested in here, whether I care about relisted dates over the last month, I care about new available over last month, exposure last month, right?
Pre-list, what have you.
I can apply this customization here.
I can even go so far as to customize which properties I want in a given report.
And then I'm gonna come over to the right-hand side.
Top right-hand corner, and I'm gonna hit an Excel report.
This, I can schedule weekly.
I can schedule it on a specific date, or the date… where's the date?
Just once, no.
So, weekly, quarterly, and, monthly.
So, every Monday, let's say, you could set at 4.
I'm gonna download this now just to give you an idea of what this report looks like.
But this might be the best way to achieve what we're looking for, because it is kind of one level of reporting down.
From what you're seeing in the data delivery reports that I emailed you.
I'm gonna reshare my screen so that the Excel is coming up.
Have you ever engaged with one of these market survey exports?
Yeah, yeah, I have.
Oh, okay, so you kind of know what I'm talking about.
Yeah, they're pretty good.
I think I just need to think about… And maybe this is something… Okay.
I think it's something we're gonna have to create with our internal BI software then, because I know we have a feed with ApartmentIQ, because really… I'll steal the screen real quick.
Yeah, let me stop.
mind.
This is what they're used to seeing, this is from Radix, it obviously looks ugly.
Download the images, just to make it look a little bit better.
But it's basically, like.
to synthesize this quickly, apartment versus… what we own, what apartment complex we own versus comp averages, right?
Rather than seeing, like, 6 different comps and, you know.
They're not as familiar with what is You know, comparable, or similar, or, similar.
Yeah.
They just kind of look at, like.
where are we versus the comps?
Are we doing better or worse than them over the last week?
Etc.
So,
let me… I appreciate you walking me through those.

I'm gonna see what I can do with our internal reporting, because I think, you know, you guys have a lot of great data, and that would be good for the… the… canned reports that you have would be great for, like, a property manager or regional manager, but I think, an executive would just… they wouldn't even look at it.
We want more of a high-level scorecard, is that kind of fair to say?
Yeah, exactly.
Rolled up averages, you know.
Okay.
Ben, would you mind if I got you in touch, perhaps?
It probably wouldn't be this week or next week, but in the next couple of weeks, if I got you in touch with our product team as we start fleshing out the portfolio report and the portfolio summaries?
Yeah, that'd be great.
Yeah, okay, because we are definitely approaching a critical point where we've got a few designs out, and I think now we're getting into the point of building this thing.
So, would love to hear your feedback.
We're working a little bit with Willowbridge as well, to kind of create this sort of reporting, so would love to get your opinion too, and make sure that this works for everybody.
Awesome.
Yeah, that would be great.
Would be happy to be a guinea pig or help throughout the process.
Appreciate that, Ben.
It's funny, I feel like when there's just, you know, sometimes you complain there's too little data, and now we're complaining that there's too much data.
It's just a funny world to be in, because I've been on both sides, having been here at ApartmentIQ now almost 3 years.
I've seen it go from, hey, there's really not enough here, to, wow, guys, like, give me a break.
Right.
I know, and it's tough because there's so many different users that…
Right.
data, right?
Like, what we… that canned reporting that you have, you know, as my background as an analyst, I'm like, great, I love that.
You know, the regionals, they want to see their sets, they don't care about anyone else.
Executives, just give me high-level numbers.
Are you doing good or bad compared to the cops?
So, it really depends on the means in which you're looking at this data.
And we definitely want to have executives bought in and using the data, so let me know as you're working and iterating on your end.

I can ask some folks on my engineering team, see if they have any ideas or any suggestions on the technical side of what other folks have done, because I'm sure, you guys aren't the only ones trying to solve for this and create those kind of roll-ups.
So, let me see what else I can find.
Thanks for sharing with me.
Okay, awesome.
Well, I will, look forward to meeting with you guys in a couple weeks, whenever that is, and we'll go from there.
Thanks, Ben.
Have a great rest of your day, alright?
We'll talk.
Yep, you as well.
Take care.
